title: Kevan Hanowski
content:
People.ContactEmail khanowski@mccarthy.ca
- British Columbia, 2017
- University of British Columbia
Solution-oriented, efficient, effective. Kevan helps clients navigate and resolve complex disputes so that they can get back to business.
A partner in our Vancouver office, Kevan’s commercial litigation and arbitration experience spans a wide range of industries, with a particular focus on financial services and class action defence. He also helps businesses to understand, navigate and challenge decisions made by all levels of government.
When litigation is unavoidable, Kevan has what it takes to go the distance. He has appeared at all levels of court in British Columbia and at the Supreme Court of Canada, and has represented clients in domestic and international arbitrations, mediations, administrative proceedings, regulatory investigations and before a provincial commission of inquiry.

#### Complex Commercial Litigation and Arbitration
Known for pragmatic advice, Kevan keeps his clients’ business and strategic objectives at the forefront. His representative commercial litigation and arbitration experience includes:
- Representing financial institutions in disputes and investigations involving (among other things) banking services, investment products, investment advisors, credit cards, and fraudulent transactions.
- Representing mining clients in civil claims and arbitrations involving mining assets and transactions around the world.
- Representing a wide range of clients in contractual disputes, shareholder disputes, and other business disputes.

#### Class Action Defence
A member of the firm’s National Class Action Group, Kevan defends complex class actions on behalf of businesses located in Canada and around the world. He  has experience at all stages of the class action life cycle – from pre-certification to trial. Kevan’s representative experience includes:
- Successfully defending a high-stakes common issues trial in a claim involving one of Canada’s largest equity mutual funds ( Turpin v TD Asset Management Inc ., 2022 BCSC 1083 ), a decision recognized with the Benchmark Litigation 2023 Impact Case Award .
- A multi-jurisdictional securities class action involving a mining company.
- Numerous class actions involving banks and financial institutions.
- A price fixing class action brought against North America’s largest beef processors.
- Product liability class actions relating to the design, development and sale of various products and consumer goods.
- Privacy class actions relating to the data sharing practices of large technology companies.
Kevan is a regional editor of the Canadian Class Actions Monitor and regularly writes about current trends in Canadian class actions.

#### Challenging Municipal and Provincial Decisions
Kevan assists clients affected by government decision‑making. He has experience with judicial reviews, constitutional challenges, and administrative and regulatory proceedings. Kevan’s representative experience includes:
- A judicial review challenging the City of Vancouver’s ability to regulate ridesharing ( Uber Canada Inc. v Vancouver (City) , 2025 BCSC 1534 ).
- A judicial review regarding an unprecedented real estate development on reserve land in downtown Vancouver ( Kits Point Residents Association v Vancouver (City) , 2023 BCSC 1706 ).
- A constitutional challenge regarding provincial power to regulate interprovincial railways ( British Columbia (Environmental Management Act) v Canadian National Railway Company, 2022 BCSC 135 ).
- A constitutional reference regarding British Columbia’s authority to regulate interprovincial pipelines ( Reference re Environmental Management Act , 2020 SCC 1 ).
- A judicial review relating to the regulation of the British Columbia real estate industry ( Superintendent of Real Estate v Real Estate Council of B.C , 2018 BCSC 1500 ).
- Representing clients before administrative boards and tribunals in British Columbia.
- Advising federally regulated clients – including banks, airlines, and railways – about division of powers issues.
